user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see my active PATs so that I can manage which apps have access.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/auth/tokens/ requires IsAuthenticated. Returns all non-revoked PATs for the authenticated user (revoked_at__isnull=True). Fields: id, name, token_prefix, scopes, last_used_at, expires_at, revoked_at, created_at.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to revoke a PAT so that the associated app loses access.
expected_behaviour: DELETE /api/v1/auth/tokens/<token_id>/ requires IsAuthenticated. Scoped to request.user (cross-user revocation impossible). 404 if not found. 400 if already revoked. Sets revoked_at=now. Returns 204.

user_story: As an external app using a PAT, I want my requests to be authenticated and scope-checked so that only authorized operations succeed.
expected_behaviour: PersonalAccessTokenAuthentication checks for 'Authorization: Bearer pat_<secret>' header. Hashes the token (SHA-256), looks up PersonalAccessToken by hash (401 if not found). Checks is_valid (not revoked, not expired) — 401 with specific message. Sets RLS context (tenant_id + user_id). Stamps last_used_at at most once per 60 seconds. Attaches pat to request.auth_pat for downstream scope checks. HasPATScope permission class checks required_scope in pat.scopes; JWT-authed requests bypass scope checks.
inventory_notes: PAT auth only tries if header starts with 'Bearer pat_' — falls through to JWT if not. last_used_at throttled to 60s to avoid cross-region write on every request.

user_story: As a subscriber who forgot their password, I want to request a reset link via email so that I can regain access.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/auth/password-reset/request/ is unauthenticated (AllowAny). Accepts {email}. Rate-limited per-IP (5/hour) and per-email (3/hour) via Django cache; 429 if either limit exceeded. Always returns 200 {detail: 'If an account exists...'} regardless of whether email exists (prevents user enumeration). If user found and is_active: generates uid (base64 user PK) + token (Django default_token_generator HMAC); sends password_reset_subject/body.txt/html templates via Django email backend. Email send failure is logged but not surfaced.
dependencies: Mailgun (email delivery via Django email backend), Django cache (rate limiting)
inventory_notes: Rate limit window is 1 hour. Fail-open on cache unavailability — rate limit is bypassed not blocked when Redis/cache is down.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to set a new password via a reset link so that I can log back in.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/auth/password-reset/confirm/ is unauthenticated (AllowAny). Accepts {uid, token, new_password}. 400 if any missing. Decodes uid (base64 user PK), looks up User (400 if invalid/not found). Checks default_token_generator.check_token() — 400 if invalid or expired (3-day default timeout). Validates new_password against Django password validators (400 with joined error messages). Calls user.set_password() (bumps password_last_changed_at), saves {password, password_last_changed_at}. Mints JWT pair via EmailTokenObtainPairSerializer.get_token() (carries pw_iat). Returns {access, refresh} 200.
inventory_notes: Must use EmailTokenObtainPairSerializer.get_token() not RefreshToken.for_user() — the latter omits pw_iat, causing immediate rejection for users with non-null password_last_changed_at.

user_story: As the billing system, I want to suspend a tenant on subscription cancellation and resume on re-subscription so that access matches payment status.
expected_behaviour: Tenant.status transitions are driven by Stripe webhooks in apps/billing/views.py (not exposed directly in tenants app). Status choices: pending, provisioning, active, suspended, deprovisioning, deleted. Active tenants must have status=active. Tenant.has_entitlement property returns True if stripe_subscription_id is set OR is_trial is True and trial_ends_at > now. Tenant.entitled_active() classmethod returns the filtered queryset used by crons and reports.
inventory_notes: Actual status transitions are in apps/billing/views.py stripe webhook handler and apps/orchestrator (provision/deprovision_tenant). The tenants app provides the model and properties.

user_story: As the system, I want to reset daily message counters at midnight UTC so that the per-day limits refresh.
expected_behaviour: reset_daily_counters_task() calls reset_daily_counters() which does Tenant.objects.filter(messages_today__gt=0).update(messages_today=0). Returns count of tenants updated. Triggered via QStash cron at midnight UTC.

user_story: As the system, I want to reset monthly usage counters on the 1st of the month so that subscribers get a fresh usage allocation each month.
expected_behaviour: reset_monthly_counters_task() calls reset_monthly_counters() which updates all tenants with messages_this_month>0: sets messages_this_month=0, tokens_this_month=0, estimated_cost_this_month=0. Also clears cost_warn_sent_at and cost_exhausted_email_sent_at on ALL tenants (unconditional). Does NOT reset purchased_credit (persists across months). Returns count of tenants with messages updated.
inventory_notes: purchased_credit is intentionally excluded — prepaid credit persists. The quota-email idempotency markers are cleared unconditionally (all tenants, not just those with usage) so fresh notifications can send in the new month.

user_story: As the system, I want to purge expired Telegram link tokens so that the database doesn't accumulate stale records.
expected_behaviour: cleanup_expired_telegram_tokens() deletes TelegramLinkToken rows where expires_at < now - 1 hour. Returns a string with the count deleted. Triggered via QStash cron.

user_story: As an operator, I want to force-rotate all user passwords and send reset emails so that user sessions are invalidated for a security hygiene event.
expected_behaviour: rotate_all_passwords management command: iterates all users except PLATFORM_OWNER_EMAIL. Skips users with no email or whose password_last_changed_at >= --since (idempotency). For each user: calls set_unusable_password() (bumps password_last_changed_at → invalidates all JWTs), saves, generates reset token, sends email (privacy_rotation_2026 template). Reports rotated/skipped/failed counts. --dry-run mode available. rotate_all_passwords_task() wraps the command for QStash dispatch.
dependencies: Mailgun (email delivery), Django cache (not used here), QStash (task dispatch)
inventory_notes: Email failure leaves the user with a rotated (unusable) password but no reset link — they are locked out until manual resend. PLATFORM_OWNER_EMAIL env var must be set or operator will be locked out.

user_story: As an operator, I want to send a promotional email campaign to eligible subscribers so that they receive trial extension offers.
expected_behaviour: send_promo_campaign management command: requires --code (campaign slug), --kind, --days, --valid_until. Looks up or creates PromoCampaign. Filters audience (non-paying, active tenants). For each user: generates HMAC-signed per-user token via promo_signing, sends email with redemption URL. Records audience snapshot on PromoCampaign. send_promo_campaign_task() wraps with hardcoded 'privacy-zdr-2026' campaign constants for QStash dispatch.

user_story: As an operator, I want to bump pending config versions for all active tenants so that their OpenClaw configs are picked up on the next apply cycle.
expected_behaviour: bump_pending_configs management command: updates all active tenants with container_id set — sets pending_config_version = config_version + 1. bump_config_version management command: accepts optional --tenant UUID. Without --tenant: bumps pending_config_version + 1 for all ACTIVE tenants. With --tenant: targets a single tenant. Both emit SUCCESS with count.
inventory_notes: bump_pending_configs uses F('config_version')+1 (relative to current); bump_config_version uses F('pending_config_version')+1 (relative to current pending). These are subtly different operations.

user_story: As an operator running migrations, I want to disable Row-Level Security on newly created tables so that the application can read and write without RLS blocking queries.
expected_behaviour: disable_rls management command: queries pg_tables for tables in non-system schemas where rowsecurity=true AND tableowner=current_user. Runs ALTER TABLE ... DISABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY for each. Skips Supabase internal tables (not owned by current_user). Reports count.
inventory_notes: Only affects tables owned by the Django DB user — Supabase internal tables (owned by 'postgres') are skipped. In prod, Django tables are owned by 'postgres' so this command may be a no-op in production (see MEMORY.md: RLS relock is effectively a no-op in prod).

user_story: As an operator, I want to see a tenant's current local time so that I can debug timezone-related issues.
expected_behaviour: tenant_local_now management command: takes tenant_id UUID argument. Looks up Tenant + user, resolves IANA timezone (falls back to UTC on invalid). Prints tenant_id, timezone, utc_time (ISO), local_time (ISO), local_date, local_time_hhmm, then 'ok'. CommandError if tenant not found.

user_story: As the OpenClaw runtime, I want to mark a welcome feature as sent so that the deploy-time backfill skips already-served tenants.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/tenants/runtime/<tenant_id>/welcomes/<feature>/ is AllowAny but requires X-NBHD-Internal-Key + X-NBHD-Tenant-Id headers (validated by validate_internal_runtime_request — 401 on failure). Valid features: fuel, finance, core (400 for others). Sets tenant.welcomes_sent[feature]=ISO timestamp. Idempotent (re-calls overwrite). Returns {feature, sent_at}. 404 if tenant not found.
inventory_notes: AllowAny permission class is intentional — auth is via the internal API key in the header, not a JWT.

user_story: As the OpenClaw runtime, I want to record engagement signals for agenda threads so that the assistant can prioritize which topics to surface.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/tenants/runtime/<tenant_id>/agenda/<kind>/<item_id>/ is AllowAny + internal API key auth. Valid kinds from AgendaEngagement.Kind. Body: {action: surfaced|abandoned|completed|active|dormant|defer, signal?: string, until?: ISO-8601}. Defer action requires 'until' field (400 if missing or invalid datetime). Maps action to service calls: mark_surfaced(), defer_until(), or mark_state()+record_signal(). Returns {kind, item_id, action}.

user_story: As the OpenClaw runtime, I want to record a future-aware commitment so that the assistant can follow up on it later.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/tenants/runtime/<tenant_id>/commitments/ is AllowAny + internal API key auth. Body: {about: string (required), surface_after: ISO-8601 (required), why: string}. Validates about and surface_after (400 if missing; 400 if surface_after invalid datetime). Calls record_commitment() which uses SHA-256 hash of about-text as item_id (idempotent — same about-text reuses the row). Creates AgendaEngagement with kind=ASSISTANT_COMMITMENT. Returns {kind, item_id, surface_after}. 404 if tenant not found.

user_story: As the OpenClaw runtime, I want to read and switch the tenant's preferred AI model so that the assistant can fulfill a user's in-chat model change request.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/tenants/runtime/<tenant_id>/preferred-model/ — internal auth required. Returns {preferred_model, applied_model, model_tier, allowed_models: [{model_id, alias}]}. POST /api/v1/tenants/runtime/<tenant_id>/preferred-model/ — internal auth required. Body: {model_id: string}. If model_id not in _get_allowed_models, returns 400 with allowed_models in body. Saves preferred_model, bumps pending_config, enqueues immediate apply. Returns {updated: True, ...state}. Empty model_id clears to tier default.
inventory_notes: Does NOT include free-offer models in the allowed set (uses TIER_MODEL_CONFIGS + _byo_model_extras only, unlike consumer PreferredModelView which also calls offer_model_entry). Minor inconsistency.
